commit.message:
refactor: extract TransactionProcessorService, add ProcessStreamJob

Pull the per-transaction compliance pipeline out of sentinel:watch into
TransactionProcessorService so the same logic is callable from both the
daemon loop and queued jobs.

ProcessStreamJob wraps the service for on-demand dispatch.
StreamTransactionsJob now publishes to the stream then dispatches
ProcessStreamJob per transaction — making the dashboard "Run Transactions"
button fully self-contained (no sentinel:watch needed).

WatchTransactions becomes a thin XREAD loop with CLI formatting only.
Docs and Anki cards updated.

...explicitly transparent. Ask me about my approach!
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix PII gate display bugs: strict block message lost, warn not visible

Bug 1: streamChunkMsg handler checked Done before processing Content,
so PII gate block messages (single chunk with Content + Done=true) were
silently dropped. Fixed by appending Content to streamBuf before the
Done check.

Bug 2: PII gate WarnFn was never wired to the TUI. Added SetWarnFn to
Gate for late binding, PIIWarnFunc in TUI that sends piiWarnMsg, and
wiring in main.go so warn-mode detections appear as system messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

completing the full top-to-bottom float-in sequence of the panel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: fix mobile LCP with font swap, image optimization, SSR fixes (#128)

* perf: fix mobile LCP by adding font swap, preconnect, image optimization

- Add display: swap to Inter font to prevent FOIT
- Add preconnect hint for Google Tag Manager
- Set hero image quality to 65 (visually identical, ~20-30% smaller)
- Enable AVIF format with WebP fallback in next.config.ts

Fixes #109

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: lazy-load NotificationChecker and PreferenceProvider

These client components only function for authenticated users but were
loading JS for all visitors including the landing page. Using dynamic
imports with ssr: false in a client wrapper defers their JS until after
initial render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: fix SSR bailout, font-display optional, eager hero image

- Keep PreferenceProvider with SSR to prevent client-rendering bailout
  that was blocking all img tags from appearing in initial HTML
- Use font-display: optional instead of swap — eliminates 132KB of
  fonts from the critical rendering path on slow connections
- NotificationChecker stays lazy-loaded (ssr: false) since it renders null
- Add explicit loading=eager on hero image

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: switch production build from Turbopack to Webpack

Turbopack produces 29% more JS (248KB vs 176KB shared) and prevents
static generation of the landing page. Webpack build produces smaller
bundles, directly reducing LCP on slow connections.

Also reverts font-display from optional back to swap to preserve
visual appearance (constraint: identical rendering).

Dev server still uses Turbopack for fast HMR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove buggy image preload and redundant loading=eager

Review found:
- imageSrcSet used & instead of & in JSX (double-encoding bug)
- Preload in root layout fires on ALL pages, not just landing
- loading="eager" is redundant with priority prop

Removed manual preload entirely — Next.js Image priority handles
page-scoped preloading. Removed redundant loading="eager".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ntfy): add upstream ntfy.sh relay for iOS push delivery

Without NTFY_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL, self-hosted ntfy has no way to reach
iOS devices via APNs. Setting it to ntfy.sh enables the relay path:
  publish → ntfy.theshire.io → ntfy.sh → APNs → iPhone

commit.message:
Add Linux ARM64 wgpu/Vulkan renderer for Asahi

Replaces the softbuffer Linux renderer with a wgpu/Vulkan backend,
mirroring the macOS wgpu/Metal backend. Fixes the black-screen freeze,
window move/resize (using native Wayland drag protocols), and
transparency (PreMultiplied alpha with shader premultiplication).

Extract shared query logic to eliminate N+1 database queries (#15)

* Add Claude Code hooks for safer, cleaner development

- PreToolUse: block dangerous commands (forced deletes, force push, SQL drops)
- PostToolUse: auto-format with Prettier after edits
- PostToolUse: run tsc --noEmit after edits to catch type errors early
- PostToolUse: warn when writing duplicate DB query patterns (N+1 guard)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Move typecheck hook to Stop event, skip when deps missing

Runs npm run build once when Claude finishes responding instead of
after every edit. Gracefully exits if node_modules aren't installed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Replace N+1 query pattern with DISTINCT ON batch queries

All 3 API routes (leaderboard, generate, analyze-repo) were running
~121 queries each (1 + 60×2) to fetch latest scores per tool.

New shared getToolsWithLatestMetrics() in lib/queries.ts uses
Postgres DISTINCT ON to fetch latest momentum scores and GitHub
snapshots in 3 total queries instead of 121.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Update tests to mock getToolsWithLatestMetrics instead of db

Tests were mocking the old N+1 db.select().from().where() chain.
Now they mock the shared query function, matching the refactored routes.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Fix type errors in queries.ts and improve typecheck hook

- Drizzle db.execute() returns RowList (array-like), not { rows }
- Use typed local variables instead of generic parameter
- Switch typecheck hook from npm run build to tsc --noEmit to avoid
  network dependencies (Google Fonts fetch) blocking the check

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Add test suite requirement to verification workflow in CLAUDE.md

Ensures npm run test is always run after code changes and that
test mocks are updated when refactoring shared code.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Update package-lock.json after npm install

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

* Split CLAUDE.md: move personal workflow/CLI docs to local config files

General development philosophy (planning, incremental changes, avoiding
AI slop) and universal CLI tools (gt, gh, npx) moved to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md.
Project-specific personal tools (neonctl, vercel, drizzle-kit) moved to
.claude/CLAUDE.md (gitignored). Repo CLAUDE.md now only has what every
contributor needs: commands, architecture, env vars, and verification.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XMpoZozw3qhffZdSmGr55i

commit.message:
Unified SVG with matching dark bg, green border, and scanlines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix theme consistency and improve network graph interpretability

Theme: add color-scheme:dark on html root; add !important to all
background rules covering stMain, stMainBlockContainer, stDecoration,
stToolbar and sidebar sub-divs; scope text color rules to .stApp h1-h5
and .stApp p/label instead of broad span/div which was breaking light
mode; force dark bg on selectbox dropdown, popover menus, inputs,
checkboxes, and alert boxes.

Graph layout: increase ForceAtlas2 repulsion gravity -55→-90, spring
length 130→170, spring strength 0.06→0.04, central gravity 0.005→0.003,
overlap avoidance 0.4→1.0 (full), stabilization iterations 150→250.
These changes spread the graph and eliminate the central blob.

Graph visual hierarchy: switch to power-scaled node sizes (range 6–56,
was 8–40) for stronger hub/peripheral distinction. Add strokeWidth=3
dark halo on labels for readability on any background. Scale edge
value to 1–8 log range instead of raw weight so thick edges don't
visually dominate.

Community color strategy: top-8 communities by member count get
distinct vivid colors; all smaller communities collapse to a single
muted #374357 tone. This makes major clusters immediately visible
instead of every node having a unique but indistinguishable color.

Neighbor focus: enable neighborhood_highlight=True on PyVis Network
so clicking any node dims all non-neighbors — built-in ego-focus mode.

Defaults: max_nodes 80→50, label_top_n 12→6 for cleaner first load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(crypto): prevent TextDecoder crash and DR session corruption on decrypt failure

The Double Ratchet decrypt() mutates session state (ratchet step, chain
key advance) BEFORE AEAD verification. If decryption fails, the session
is permanently corrupted and subsequent attempts produce garbage bytes
that crash TextDecoder with "The encoded data was not valid."

Three targeted fixes:
- Add decodeAndParsePayload() that validates first-byte is JSON ({/[),
  uses TextDecoder with fatal:true, and wraps in try-catch with
  descriptive errors instead of crashing
- Snapshot DR session via serialize/deserialize before decrypt; roll back
  on AEAD or decode failure so the session remains usable for retries
- For initial DM messages (0x01), avoid caching a corrupted session if
  decrypt or decode fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: adapt extension to YouTube dark/light theme (#87)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix daily data: EOD + 1H extended overlay for full-session OHLC

The 1-hour-only approach caused data truncation over 500-day lookback
(~8000 bars). Now uses a hybrid approach:
1. EOD endpoint for full history (reliable, no truncation)
2. 1-hour extended endpoint for last 15 days only
3. Merges: takes max(eod.high, ext.high), min(eod.low, ext.low)
   and extended session open/close for recent candles

This captures pre-market/after-hours moves for A/X classification
while keeping full history for monthly/quarterly analysis.

Also: hides partial badge, fixes todayISO to use ET date instead
of UTC (prevents edge case during EST winter months).

fix: bust browser cache after avatar/banner upload

The avatar/banner URL never changes (/api/v1/users/{id}/avatar), so
browsers serve the cached old image after uploading a new one. Append
a cache-busting ?t= query param after upload so the browser refetches.
Also reset Avatar imgError state when the URL changes so a previously
failed load doesn't stick after a successful upload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tunnel forwarding via XDP_PASS + TC egress passthrough

Two issues prevented transit traffic from being forwarded through
GRE/ESP tunnel interfaces:

1. redirect_capable was set for tunnel interfaces, causing
   bpf_redirect_map to send Ethernet-framed packets into POINTOPOINT
   tunnels that expect raw IP. The tunnel driver encapsulated the
   full buffer including the 14-byte Ethernet header, producing
   corrupted GRE payloads (inner "IP [version 0 != 4]").

   Fix: exclude tunnel interfaces (IFACE_FLAG_TUNNEL) from both
   redirect_capable map and tx_ports devmap. XDP falls back to
   XDP_PASS, letting the kernel strip Ethernet and route through
   the tunnel correctly.

2. TC egress dropped tunnel-encapsulated outer packets. When XDP
   does XDP_PASS for tunnel-routed traffic, the kernel forwards
   through the tunnel driver which prepends outer headers to the
   same skb — preserving ingress_ifindex from the original inner
   packet. TC egress saw non-zero ingress_ifindex with no session
   and dropped the outer packet (TC_ACT_SHOT).

   Fix: allow GRE (proto 47) and ESP (proto 50) outer packets
   through TC egress when ingress_ifindex is set. The inner packet
   was already validated by the XDP pipeline; the outer
   encapsulation is trusted local kernel work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: detect and prevent stuck shepherd sessions (#138)

* fix: detect and prevent stuck shepherd sessions

ShepherdSession::reader_alive() was hardcoded to return true, so socket
disconnections were never detected. The shepherd also used non-blocking
writes that could fail with WouldBlock during output bursts, dropping
the client connection. Together these caused sessions to become visible
but not interactive — input silently dropped, no output flowing.

Fix both sides: add reader_alive flag tracking to ShepherdSession
(mirroring the existing Session pattern), and switch shepherd writes
to blocking mode with a 5-second timeout to prevent WouldBlock-induced
disconnections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use poll-for-writability instead of flipflopping socket blocking mode

Keep the client socket consistently non-blocking. On WouldBlock during
writes, poll for writability with a 5-second deadline instead of
temporarily switching to blocking mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add disconnection test coverage for shepherd and session

- write_input_ok_after_disconnect: broken pipe swallowed on input write
- resize_ok_after_disconnect: broken pipe swallowed on resize
- write_message_to_closed_peer_fails: shepherd write errors on dead socket

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve diagnostics, output reliability, and risk docs for peer-review

Cherry-picked from a self-review (/peer-review on SKILL.md itself):

1. Stderr capture: Replace 2>/dev/null with temp file capture so CLI
   failures (auth errors, rate limits) show diagnostic output instead
   of silent "exit code 1" messages.

2. Version/auth preflight: Add --version smoke checks after command -v
   to catch OAuth/auth issues before parallel dispatch hangs.

3. Numbered output: Append "Number each finding sequentially" to all
   20 multi-round mode prompts (10 modes x 2 models) so the cherry-pick
   UX ("keep 1, 3, 5") works reliably.

4. Accepted Risks: Document the argv prompt exposure limitation with
   mitigations and future fix path.
